info.title: tesseract-rs OCR
info.description: Pure-Rust, byte-parity Tesseract OCR (English + German models) exposed as a Power Platform custom connector. Recognize a document image into structured tesseract-rs/doc.v1 JSON (text/table/figure/header/footer regions, reconstructed table cell grids, per-word confidence, typed field harvest), or export a searchable or structured PDF. Zero C libraries at runtime. See https://github.com/AdaWorldAPI/tesseract-rs.

api_key.name: x-api-key
api_key.description: Optional shared-secret gate. Only enforced when the deployment sets the TESSERACT_API_KEY environment variable; otherwise every request is accepted (the server's default, open demo mode). See integrations/power-platform/README.md.

post.description: Decodes an image (PNG/JPEG/WebP/TIFF/GIF/BMP/PNM) and returns the structured tesseract-rs/doc.v1 document: text/table/figure/header/footer regions, reconstructed table cell grids, per-word confidence, and a typed field harvest (amounts, IBANs, GUIDs). Primary input is the raw image bytes (application/octet-stream) - this matches Microsoft Graph's 'Get file content' output byte-for-byte, so a SharePoint/OneDrive/Outlook-attachment flow can pass the file content straight through with no conversion step. lang selects the recognition model: eng (default) or deu; an unrecognized value falls back to eng rather than erroring. For a binary body, pass lang via the ?lang= query parameter (there is no body field to carry it); for a JSON body, send {"content_base64": "<base64>", "lang": "eng"} instead (the JSON field wins if both are somehow present).

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Recognition model for a binary (application/octet-stream) body. Ignored when the body is application/json - use the JSON form's own lang field there instead.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: Raw image bytes (application/octet-stream), e.g. the binary output of a 'Get file content' action. When Content-Type is application/json instead, send {"content_base64": "...", "lang": "eng"} (see the RecognizeJsonBody definition).

200.description: The recognized document as tesseract-rs/doc.v1 JSON.

400.description: The image could not be decoded, was too large/small, or the JSON body was malformed.

401.description: Missing or invalid x-api-key header (only enforced when TESSERACT_API_KEY is configured server-side).

503.description: The server is shutting down and is not accepting new recognition work.

post.description: Recognizes the input image and renders a PDF. Default: the original scan raster with an invisible, selectable text layer (mode=searchable). Pass ?mode=structured for the doc.v1-driven layout reconstruction instead - equivalent to calling the dedicated StructuredPdf action below. Same input shapes as RecognizeDocument (binary, or {content_base64, lang} JSON); lang selects eng (default) or deu the same way.

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: PDF reconstruction mode. searchable (default): original raster + invisible text layer. structured: doc.v1 layout reconstruction (same as the StructuredPdf action).

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Recognition model for a binary (application/octet-stream) body. Ignored when the body is application/json - use the JSON form's own lang field there instead.

400.description: The image could not be decoded, was too large/small, or the JSON body was malformed.

401.description: Missing or invalid x-api-key header (only enforced when TESSERACT_API_KEY is configured server-side).

503.description: The server is shutting down and is not accepting new recognition work.

post.description: A query-param-free-for-mode alias for POST /api/v1/pdf?mode=structured, exposed as its own action so it is directly selectable in the Power Automate action picker. Renders the doc.v1 layout reconstruction ('B'): regions, tables, and figures laid out from the recognized structure rather than the original scan raster. Same input shapes as RecognizeDocument; ?lang= is still accepted (there is no mode to select here, but the language switch is orthogonal).

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Recognition model for a binary (application/octet-stream) body. Ignored when the body is application/json - use the JSON form's own lang field there instead.

400.description: The image could not be decoded, was too large/small, or the JSON body was malformed.

401.description: Missing or invalid x-api-key header (only enforced when TESSERACT_API_KEY is configured server-side).

503.description: The server is shutting down and is not accepting new recognition work.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded image bytes (standard or URL-safe alphabet, padded or not).

lang.description: Selects the recognition model: eng (default) or deu. An unrecognized value falls back to eng rather than erroring. deu is only available when the deployment's model directory has the deu.lstm* components; otherwise a deu request also falls back to eng.

BBox.description: [left, top, right, bottom] in image pixels, top-down origin.

numeric_norm.description: Present only when numeric hardening changed the recognized text (e.g. OCR'd 'O' corrected to '0').

type.description: text | table | figure | header | footer | paragraph. Additive: ignore unrecognized values.

cells.description: Table regions only: the reconstructed cell grid.

value_cents.description: Present when the field is a hardened monetary amount.

mean_conf.description: Mean word confidence 0-100, or null when the page has no words.

low_confidence.description: true suggests handwriting, low resolution, or non-printed input.

DocV1.description: tesseract-rs/doc.v1 - the canonical structured OCR document.
